title: Rape of 9 Year Old
hook: A 9-year-old girl's life was forever changed in a brutal act of violence. The perpetrator's shocking confession would send shockwaves through the community.
background: In Everton, Gauteng, a young girl named Lebohang Moshue lived a life that would take a devastating turn. The accused, Mojalefa Elias Tsotetsi, would commit a heinous crime that would haunt the community. The incident occurred on February 5, 2014, after a feast where Tsotetsi was tasked with taking a tent to his mother's place, accompanied by the young victim.
incident: As they walked, Tsotetsi decided to have sexual intercourse with the child, taking her into the veldt and raping her. After the horrific act, he took her home, leaving the child traumatized. The crime was reported, and Tsotetsi was arrested and charged with rape. He pleaded guilty, submitting a statement detailing the events of that fateful day.
climax: The case took a dramatic turn when Tsotetsi appealed his sentence, arguing that life imprisonment was not appropriate in every instance of rape, especially considering the circumstances of the case. He cited comparative case law, including the case of S v Abrahams, which stated that some rapes are worse than others and that life sentences should be reserved for cases devoid of substantial mitigating factors.
verdict: The court ultimately upheld the sentence of life imprisonment, determining that the circumstances of the case justified the maximum penalty. Tsotetsi's appeal was denied, and he was sentenced to life in prison for the horrific crime he committed.

image_prompts: A close-up shot of a child's face with a blurred background, conveying a sense of fear and vulnerability
A wide shot of a rural area in Everton, Gauteng, with a sense of isolation and desolation
A shot of a veldt, with a sense of eeriness and foreboding, symbolizing the location of the crime
